description: Curated Suricata rule examples from official AWS and Suricata documentation

examples.name: Allow SSM endpoints via TLS SNI
examples.rule: pass tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (ssl_state:client_hello; tls.sni; content:"ssm."; startswith; content:".amazonaws.com"; endswith; nocase; flow:to_server; sid:202308311;)
examples.description: Allow access to any domain beginning with ssm. and ending with .amazonaws.com

examples.name: Allow specific JA3 hash (Curl 7.79.1)
examples.rule: pass tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"Only allow Curl 7.79.1 JA3"; ja3.hash; content:"27e9c7cc45ae47dc50f51400db8a4099"; sid:12820009;)
examples.description: Allow outbound access using a specific JA3 client fingerprint hash

examples.name: Allow DNS queries for amazonaws.com
examples.rule: pass dns $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (dns.query; dotprefix; content:".amazonaws.com"; endswith; nocase; msg:"Pass rules do not alert"; sid:118947;)
examples.description: Allow DNS queries for domain names ending in .amazonaws.com

examples.name: Allow AWS SFTP banner only
examples.rule: pass ssh $EXTERNAL_NET 22 -> $HOME_NET any (ssh.software; content:"AWS_SFTP"; flow:from_server; sid:217872;)
examples.description: Allow only SSH/SFTP servers with AWS_SFTP banner

examples.name: Block old TLS versions
examples.rule: reject tls any any -> any any (msg:"TLS 1.0 or 1.1"; ssl_version:tls1.0,tls1.1; sid:2023070518;)
examples.description: Block connections using TLS version 1.0 or 1.1

examples.name: Block multiple CIDR ranges
examples.rule: drop ip $HOME_NET any -> [10.10.0.0/16,10.11.0.0/16,10.12.0.0/16] any (msg:"Block traffic to multiple CIDRs"; sid:278970;)
examples.description: Block outbound access to multiple CIDR ranges in a single rule

examples.name: Block multiple SNIs via PCRE
examples.rule: reject tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (ssl_state:client_hello; tls.sni; pcre:"/(example1\.com|example2\.com)$/i"; flow:to_server; msg:"Domain blocked"; sid:1457;)
examples.description: Block multiple SNIs with a single rule using PCRE

examples.name: Block high-risk destination ports
examples.rule: drop ip $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET [1389,53,4444,445,135,139,389,3389] (msg:"Deny List High Risk Destination Ports"; sid:278670;)
examples.description: Block multiple high-risk destination outbound ports

examples.name: Block HTTP direct-to-IP
examples.rule: reject http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (http.host; content:"."; pcre:"/^(?:[0-9]{1,3}\\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}$/"; msg:"IP in HTTP HOST Header"; flow:to_server; sid:1239847;)
examples.description: Block outbound HTTP connections with IP address in HOST header

examples.name: Block TLS direct-to-IP via SNI
examples.rule: reject tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (ssl_state:client_hello; tls.sni; content:"."; pcre:"/^(?:[0-9]{1,3}\\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}$/"; msg:"IP in TLS SNI"; flow:to_server; sid:1239848;)
examples.description: Block outbound TLS connections with IP address in SNI

examples.name: Block non-TCP/UDP/ICMP protocols
examples.rule: drop ip any any -> any any (noalert; ip_proto:!TCP; ip_proto:!UDP; ip_proto:!ICMP; sid:21801620;)
examples.description: Silently block any IP protocols other than TCP, UDP, and ICMP

examples.name: Block SSH on non-standard ports
examples.rule: reject ssh $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET !22 (msg:"Block use of SSH protocol on non-standard port"; flow:to_server; sid:2171010;)
examples.description: Block SSH protocol on non-standard ports

examples.name: Block non-SSH on port 22
examples.rule: reject tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 22 (msg:"Block TCP/22 servers that are not SSH protocol"; flow:to_server; app-layer-protocol:!ssh; sid:2171009;)
examples.description: Block TCP/22 servers not using SSH protocol

examples.name: Log allowed SNI with alert
examples.rule: pass tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (ssl_state:client_hello; tls.sni; content:".example.com"; dotprefix; endswith; nocase; alert; sid:202307052;)
examples.description: Log all passed traffic to an allowed SNI using alert keyword in pass rule

examples.name: Default egress TCP block with logging
examples.rule: reject tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"Default Egress TCP block to server"; flow:to_server; sid:202308171;)
examples.description: Log direction of denied traffic in default-deny policy

examples.name: SNI allowlist with cert issuer check (xbits)
examples.rule: alert tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (ssl_state:client_hello; tls.sni; content:"checkip.amazonaws.com"; endswith; nocase; xbits:set,allowed_sni_destination_ips,track ip_dst,expire 3600; noalert; sid:238745;)
examples.description: Set xbit on allowed SNI for subsequent cert issuer verification

examples.name: HTTP Content-Length check (large POST >1MB)
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"HTTP Content-Length Request Byte Test Example"; flow:established,to_server; http.method; content:"POST"; http.content_len; byte_test:0,>=,1048576,0,string,dec; sid:99; rev:1;)
examples.description: Alert on HTTP POST requests with Content-Length >= 1MB using byte_test for numeric comparison

examples.name: HTTP request with full header matching
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"HTTP Request Example"; flow:established,to_server; http.method; content:"GET"; http.uri; content:"/index.html"; bsize:11; http.protocol; content:"HTTP/1.1"; bsize:8; http.user_agent; content:"Mozilla/5.0"; bsize:11; http.host; content:"suricata.io"; bsize:11; sid:25; rev:1;)
examples.description: Full HTTP request matching with method, URI, protocol, user-agent, and host

examples.name: HTTP anomaly detection with flowint
examples.rule: alert http any any -> any any (msg:"SURICATA HTTP unknown error"; flow:established; app-layer-event:http.unknown_error; flowint:http.anomaly.count,+,1; classtype:protocol-command-decode; sid:2221000; rev:1;)
examples.description: Built-in Suricata rule using app-layer-event and flowint for HTTP anomaly counting

examples.name: TLS heartbleed detection
examples.rule: alert tls any any -> any any (msg:"SURICATA TLS overflow heartbeat encountered, possible exploit attempt (heartbleed)"; flow:established; app-layer-event:tls.overflow_heartbeat_message; flowint:tls.anomaly.count,+,1; classtype:protocol-command-decode; reference:cve,2014-0160; sid:2230012; rev:1;)
examples.description: Built-in Suricata rule detecting TLS heartbleed exploit using CVE reference

examples.name: DNS domain matching with dotprefix transform
examples.rule: alert dns any any -> any any (dns.query; dotprefix; content:".microsoft.com"; endswith; sid:1;)
examples.description: Use dotprefix transform to match subdomains of microsoft.com without false positives

examples.name: SSH software version detection
examples.rule: alert ssh any any -> any any (msg:"match SSH software string"; ssh.software; content:"openssh"; nocase; sid:1000020;)
examples.description: Match SSH connections by software banner using ssh.software sticky buffer

examples.name: TLS SNI domain blocking
examples.rule: alert tls any any -> any any (msg:"Block malicious TLS domain"; flow:established,to_server; tls.sni; content:"malware.com"; nocase; isdataat:!1,relative; sid:1000030; rev:1;)
examples.description: Block TLS connections to specific domain using tls.sni with exact match (isdataat:!1,relative ensures no trailing data)

examples.name: DNS query detection
examples.rule: alert dns any any -> any any (msg:"DNS query for suspicious domain"; dns.query; content:"malware"; nocase; sid:1000040; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect DNS queries containing suspicious keywords using dns.query sticky buffer

examples.name: File type detection by extension
examples.rule: alert http any any -> any any (msg:"Executable file download detected"; flow:established,to_client; file.name; content:".exe"; nocase; endswith; sid:1000050; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect executable file downloads using file.name sticky buffer with endswith

examples.name: Large file transfer detection
examples.rule: alert http any any -> any any (msg:"Large file upload over 100MB"; flow:established,to_server; filesize:>100MB; sid:1000060; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect large file uploads using filesize keyword with MB unit

examples.name: Rate limiting with threshold
examples.rule: alert tcp !$HOME_NET any -> $HOME_NET 25 (msg:"Possible spambot - frequent emails"; flow:established; content:"mail from|3a|"; nocase; threshold: type threshold, track by_src, count 10, seconds 60; classtype:misc-activity; sid:2002087; rev:10;)
examples.description: Alert after 10+ inbound emails from same source within 60 seconds using threshold keyword

examples.name: SSH blacklist with xbits
examples.rule: drop ssh any any -> any 22 (msg:"DROP libssh incoming"; flow:to_server,established; ssh.software; content:"libssh"; xbits:set, badssh, track ip_src, expire 3600; sid:4000000005;)
examples.description: Drop SSH connections from bots using libssh and set xbit for IP blacklisting

examples.name: AWS: Port/protocol enforcement - TLS on 443 only
examples.rule: reject tcp $HOME_NET any -> any 443 (msg:"Egress Port TCP/443 but not TLS"; app-layer-protocol:!tls; flow:to_server; sid:202501030;)
examples.description: Reject non-TLS traffic on port 443 using app-layer-protocol negation

examples.name: AWS: Block direct-to-IP HTTP connections
examples.rule: reject http $HOME_NET any -> any any (http.host; content:"."; pcre:"/^(?:[0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}$/"; msg:"HTTP direct to IP"; flow:to_server; sid:202501026;)
examples.description: Block HTTP requests where host header is an IP address (common malware technique)

examples.name: AWS: GeoIP blocking - Russia
examples.rule: drop ip $HOME_NET any -> any any (msg:"Egress traffic to RU IP"; geoip:dst,RU; metadata:geo RU; flow:to_server; sid:202501028;)
examples.description: Block all egress traffic to Russian IP addresses using geoip keyword

examples.name: AWS: Block high-risk ccTLD via TLS SNI
examples.rule: reject tls $HOME_NET any -> any any (tls.sni; content:".ru"; nocase; endswith; msg:"Egress traffic to RU ccTLD"; flow:to_server; sid:202501036;)
examples.description: Block TLS connections to .ru domains using SNI endswith matching

examples.name: AWS: Allow AWS service endpoint via TLS SNI
examples.rule: pass tls $HOME_NET any -> any any (tls.sni; content:"ssm."; startswith; nocase; content:".amazonaws.com"; endswith; nocase; flow:to_server; sid:202501048;)
examples.description: Allow SSM service endpoint using startswith/endswith content matching on TLS SNI

examples.name: AWS: Allow domain and all subdomains with dotprefix
examples.rule: pass tls $HOME_NET any -> any any (tls.sni; dotprefix; content:".amazon.com"; nocase; endswith; flow:to_server; sid:202501078;)
examples.description: Allow amazon.com and all subdomains using dotprefix transform (note leading dot)

examples.name: AWS: Alert before pass for logging allowed traffic
examples.rule: alert tls $HOME_NET any -> any any (tls.sni; content:".amazonaws.com"; nocase; endswith; msg:"*.amazonaws.com allowed"; flow:to_server; sid:021420241;)
examples.description: Alert rule placed before pass rule to log allowed traffic (pass rules don't log)

examples.name: AWS: Block QUIC traffic
examples.rule: drop quic $HOME_NET any -> any any (msg:"QUIC traffic blocked"; flow:to_server; sid:3898932;)
examples.description: Block all QUIC/HTTP3 traffic to force TLS inspection on TCP

examples.name: AWS: Cost-optimized SSH alert with threshold
examples.rule: alert ssh $HOME_NET any -> any any (msg:"Egress SSH - alert only once every ten minutes"; threshold: type limit, track by_both, seconds 600, count 1; flow:to_server; sid:898233;)
examples.description: Rate-limited SSH alert to reduce logging costs using threshold type limit

examples.name: AWS: Block malicious domain category
examples.rule: reject tls $HOME_NET any -> any any (msg:"Category:Malware"; aws_domain_category:Malware; ja4.hash; content:"_"; flow:to_server; sid:202602064;)
examples.description: Block TLS connections to AWS-categorized malware domains using managed domain categories

examples.name: AWS: TCP 3-way handshake pass for domain filtering
examples.rule: pass tcp $HOME_NET any -> any any (flow:not_established, to_server; sid:202501021;)
examples.description: Allow TCP handshake setup so domain filtering rules can inspect application layer

examples.name: AWS: Default egress HTTPS reject
examples.rule: reject tls $HOME_NET any -> any any (msg:"Default Egress HTTPS Reject"; ssl_state:client_hello; ja4.hash; content:"_"; flowbits:set,blocked; flow:to_server; sid:999991;)
examples.description: Default block rule for egress HTTPS using ssl_state and flowbits coordination

examples.name: AWS: High risk port monitoring
examples.rule: alert ip $HOME_NET any -> any [4444,666,3389] (msg:"Egress traffic to high risk port!"; flow:to_server; sid:202501058;)
examples.description: Alert on egress traffic to commonly abused ports (Metasploit, RDP, etc.)

examples.name: AWS: JA4 No-SNI reject
examples.rule: reject tls $HOME_NET any -> any any (ja4.hash; content:"_"; startswith; content:!"d"; offset:3; depth:1; msg:"JA4 No SNI Reject"; sid:1297713;)
examples.description: Reject TLS connections with no SNI set. The JA4 hash encodes whether SNI is present — this rule detects its absence using byte-level matching on the JA4 hash structure.

examples.name: DNS tunneling - excessive query volume
examples.rule: alert udp $HOME_NET any -> any 53 (msg:"Excessive DNS queries - possible DNS tunneling"; flow:to_server; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 50, seconds 120; sid:2000001; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect high DNS query rate from single host using threshold with track by_src (not by_both)

examples.name: DNS tunneling - long subdomain exfiltration
examples.rule: alert dns $HOME_NET any -> any 53 (msg:"Abnormally long DNS subdomain - potential data exfiltration"; flow:to_server; dns.query; pcre:"/^[^.]{40,}/"; sid:2000002; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect DNS queries with subdomain labels exceeding 40 chars using pcre (not isdataat for subdomain length)

examples.name: DNS tunneling - large TXT response C2
examples.rule: alert dns any 53 -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"Large DNS TXT response - possible C2 communication"; flow:established,to_client; dsize:>200; content:"|00 10|"; sid:2000003; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect large DNS TXT record responses. Source is DNS server on port 53 (responses come FROM port 53). Uses dsize for payload size and content match for TXT record type 16

examples.name: TLS C2 - suspicious certificate issuer (not well-known CA)
examples.rule: alert tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (msg:"Suspicious TLS certificate issuer - possible C2"; flow:established,to_server; tls.cert_issuer; pcre:"/^(?!.*(?:DigiCert|Let's Encrypt|Comodo|GlobalSign|Sectigo|Amazon|Google Trust|Microsoft|IdenTrust|Baltimore|Entrust|GeoTrust|Thawte|VeriSign|Go Daddy|Starfield|USERTrust|Buypass|QuoVadis|SwissSign|ISRG Root))/i"; sid:3000010; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect TLS certificates NOT from well-known CAs using negative PCRE lookahead on tls.cert_issuer. Suricata CANNOT compare subject==issuer natively. Do NOT use both tls.cert_subject and tls.cert_issuer in the same rule — the second sticky buffer overrides the first.

examples.name: TLS C2 - uncommon issuer with short CN (ephemeral C2 cert)
examples.rule: alert tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (msg:"Suspicious TLS cert from uncommon issuer with short CN - possible ephemeral C2"; flow:established,to_server; tls.cert_issuer; content:"CN="; pcre:"/^CN=[a-zA-Z0-9\\-\\.]{1,30}(,\\s*O=[^,]{1,30})?$/"; pcre:"/^(?!.*(?:DigiCert|Let's Encrypt|Comodo|GlobalSign|Sectigo|Amazon|Google Trust|Microsoft|IdenTrust))/i"; sid:3000014; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect certs from non-well-known issuers with short CN values typical of auto-generated C2 certificates. Approximation for short-lived cert detection since tls_cert_notbefore/tls_cert_notafter are NOT valid rule keywords.

examples.name: TLS C2 - weak cipher suite NULL encryption
examples.rule: alert tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (msg:"Weak TLS cipher suite detected - NULL encryption"; flow:to_server; ssl_state:client_hello; tls.ciphersuites; content:"|00 00|"; sid:3000011; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect TLS ClientHello offering NULL cipher suite (0x0000). Uses ssl_state:client_hello then tls.ciphersuites sticky buffer before content match.

examples.name: TLS C2 - weak cipher suite with PCRE alternation
examples.rule: alert tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (msg:"Weak or uncommon TLS cipher suite - possible C2 evasion"; flow:to_server; ssl_state:client_hello; tls.ciphersuites; pcre:"/\\x00\\x00|\\x00\\x03/"; sid:3000015; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect NULL (0x0000) OR EXPORT_RC4_40 (0x0003) cipher suites using PCRE alternation after tls.ciphersuites sticky buffer. Use pcre for OR matching multiple ciphers.

examples.name: TLS C2 - periodic beaconing small payloads
examples.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (msg:"Periodic beaconing detected - possible C2 check-in"; flow:established,to_server; dsize:<1024; app-layer-protocol:tls; threshold:type threshold, track by_both, count 5, seconds 300; sid:3000012; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect periodic small-payload connections to port 443. Uses flow:established (NOT flags:S) because dsize requires payload data. Uses track by_both for src-dst pair tracking. Do NOT use dsize with SYN packets.

examples.name: TLS C2 - certificate from uncommon issuer with xbits
examples.rule: alert tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (msg:"TLS cert from uncommon issuer"; flow:established,to_server; tls.cert_issuer; pcre:"/^(?!.*(?:DigiCert|Let's Encrypt|Comodo|GlobalSign|Sectigo|Amazon|Google Trust))/i"; sid:3000013; rev:1;)
examples.description: Alert on TLS certificates NOT issued by well-known CAs using negative lookahead PCRE. Useful for C2 detection where attackers use self-signed or obscure CA certs.

examples.name: Protocol anomaly - HTTP cleartext on port 443
examples.rule: alert http any any -> any 443 (msg:"HTTP clear text on port 443"; flow:to_server; app-layer-protocol:http; threshold: type limit, track by_src, seconds 3600, count 1; sid:2271019; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect unencrypted HTTP on port 443 (should be TLS). Uses app-layer-protocol:http to match actual protocol, not just port.

examples.name: Protocol anomaly - non-HTTP on TCP port 80
examples.rule: alert tcp any any -> any 80 (msg:"Non-HTTP on TCP port 80"; flow:to_server; app-layer-protocol:!http; threshold: type limit, track by_src, seconds 60, count 1; sid:2271002; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect non-HTTP traffic on port 80 using app-layer-protocol negation. Catches tunneling or protocol abuse.

examples.name: Protocol anomaly - TLS on unusual port
examples.rule: alert tcp any any -> any ![443,465,587] (msg:"TLS on unusual port"; flow:to_server; app-layer-protocol:tls; threshold: type limit, track by_src, seconds 60, count 1; sid:2271004; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect TLS on non-standard ports using port negation group and app-layer-protocol:tls. Catches C2 or data exfiltration over encrypted channels on unusual ports.

examples.name: Protocol anomaly - non-TLS on TLS port
examples.rule: alert tcp any any -> any [443,465] (msg:"Non-TLS on TLS port"; flow:to_server; app-layer-protocol:!tls; threshold: type limit, track by_src, seconds 60, count 1; sid:2271003; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect non-TLS traffic on ports 443/465 using app-layer-protocol negation. Catches protocol abuse or tunneling.

examples.name: Protocol anomaly - SSH on unusual port
examples.rule: alert tcp any any -> any !22 (msg:"SSH on unusual port"; flow:to_server; app-layer-protocol:ssh; threshold: type limit, track by_src, seconds 60, count 1; sid:2271009; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect SSH on non-standard ports. Catches SSH tunneling or backdoor access on unexpected ports.

examples.name: Protocol anomaly - non-SSH on TCP port 22
examples.rule: alert tcp any any -> any 22 (msg:"Non-SSH on TCP port 22"; flow:to_server; app-layer-protocol:!ssh; threshold: type limit, track by_src, seconds 60, count 1; sid:2271010; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect non-SSH traffic on port 22. Catches protocol abuse or tunneling through SSH port.

examples.name: Protocol anomaly - FTP on unusual port
examples.rule: alert tcp any any -> any ![20,21,6667] (msg:"FTP on unusual TCP port"; flow:to_server; app-layer-protocol:ftp; threshold: type limit, track by_src, seconds 60, count 1; sid:2271005; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect FTP on non-standard ports using port negation group.

examples.name: Protocol anomaly - DNS-TCP on unusual port
examples.rule: alert tcp any any -> any !53 (msg:"DNS-TCP on unusual port"; flow:to_server; app-layer-protocol:dns; threshold: type limit, track by_src, seconds 60, count 1; sid:2271015; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect DNS over TCP on non-standard ports. May indicate DNS tunneling or exfiltration.

examples.name: Protocol anomaly - DNS-UDP on unusual port
examples.rule: alert udp any any -> any !53 (msg:"DNS-UDP on unusual port"; flow:to_server; app-layer-protocol:dns; threshold: type limit, track by_src, seconds 60, count 1; sid:2271017; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect DNS over UDP on non-standard ports. May indicate DNS tunneling.

examples.name: HTTP on unusual port
examples.rule: alert tcp any any -> any !80 (msg:"HTTP on unusual port"; flow:to_server; app-layer-protocol:http; threshold: type limit, track by_src, seconds 60, count 1; sid:2271001; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect HTTP on non-standard ports. Catches web shells, C2 callbacks, or misconfigured services.

examples.name: Suspicious domain - Tor exit node via HTTP
examples.rule: alert http any any -> any any (msg:"HTTP Request to suspicious *.onion.to domain"; flow:established,to_server; content:".onion.to"; http_host; isdataat:!1,relative; sid:2500007; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect HTTP requests to Tor-to-web proxy domains (.onion.to). Uses http_host with isdataat:!1,relative for exact domain suffix match.

examples.name: Suspicious domain - generic .onion proxy
examples.rule: alert http any any -> any any (msg:"HTTP Request to suspicious *.onion.* domain"; flow:established,to_server; content:".onion."; http_host; isdataat:!1,relative; sid:2500011; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect HTTP requests to any Tor-to-web proxy domain (.onion.*). Broad pattern for Tor proxy detection.

examples.name: Suspicious DNS query - .ws TLD
examples.rule: alert udp any any -> any 53 (msg:"DNS Query to suspicious *.ws domain"; content:"|01 00 00 01 00 00 00 00 00 00|"; depth:10; offset:2; content:"|02|ws|00|"; nocase; sid:2500003; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect DNS queries to .ws TLD using raw DNS packet content matching with hex byte patterns for DNS wire format.

examples.name: Plaintext private key over HTTP
examples.rule: alert http any any -> any any (msg:"Plaintext PEM RSA private key over HTTP"; filemagic:"PEM RSA private key"; sid:2500017; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect RSA private keys transmitted in plaintext over HTTP using filemagic keyword for file type detection.

examples.name: Plaintext certificate request over HTTP
examples.rule: alert http any any -> any any (msg:"Plaintext PEM certificate request over HTTP"; filemagic:"PEM certificate request"; sid:2500016; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect PEM certificate requests transmitted over HTTP using filemagic keyword.

examples.name: Pass rule - Windows Update whitelist
examples.rule: pass http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (flow:established,to_server; content:"windowsupdate.com"; http_host; isdataat:!1,relative; sid:9000001; rev:1;)
examples.description: Bypass detection engine for Windows Update traffic. Pass rules prevent matched traffic from triggering other rules. Use sparingly.

examples.name: Backdoor - Teleconsole DNS query
examples.rule: alert dns any any -> any any (msg:"Teleconsole DNS query"; dns_query; content:"teleconsole"; nocase; sid:9000032; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect DNS queries for Teleconsole remote access tool using dns_query content match.

examples.name: Suspicious TLS cert - Tor onion.to
examples.rule: alert tls any any -> any any (msg:"SSL session to suspicious *onion.to"; tls.subject:"CN=*.onion.to, OU=Domain Control Validated"; sid:2500013; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect TLS connections with certificates issued to Tor proxy domains using tls.subject exact match.

examples.name: SSH brute force detection with SYN flags
examples.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> any 22 (msg:"Potential SSH brute force attack from internal host"; flow:to_server; flags:S; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 10, seconds 60; sid:1000005; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect SSH brute force by counting SYN packets (connection attempts). MUST use flags:S with flow:to_server, NOT flow:established — established only matches completed handshakes and misses failed login attempts.

examples.name: SSH tunneling detection with stream_size and app-layer-protocol
examples.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> any any (msg:"Suspicious SSH tunnel - high data volume"; flow:established,to_server; app-layer-protocol:ssh; stream_size:server,>,10485760; sid:1000006; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect SSH tunneling by monitoring data volume. MUST include app-layer-protocol:ssh to scope to SSH only (without it, matches ALL TCP traffic over 10MB). Uses stream_size for byte counting.

examples.name: TLS C2 - periodic beaconing with small payloads
examples.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (msg:"Periodic beaconing detected - possible automated C2 check-in"; flow:established,to_server; app-layer-protocol:tls; dsize:<1024; threshold:type threshold, track by_both, count 5, seconds 300; sid:3000003; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect periodic small-payload TLS connections. Uses flow:established (NOT flags:S — SYN packets have no payload so dsize won't work). Uses track by_both for src-dst pair tracking. app-layer-protocol:tls scopes to TLS only.

examples.name: HTTP/2 Transfer-Encoding smuggling (RFC 7540 violation)
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (msg:"Transfer-Encoding header in HTTP/2 - protocol violation for smuggling"; flow:established,to_server; http.protocol; content:"HTTP/2"; http.header_names; content:"Transfer-Encoding"; nocase; sid:4000002; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect Transfer-Encoding header in HTTP/2 traffic (forbidden by RFC 7540). Uses http.protocol to match HTTP/2, then http.header_names to check for header presence. Do NOT use http.header for presence checks — use http.header_names. Do NOT add CRLF hex bytes in header_names content.

examples.name: WebSocket upgrade with IP-based Origin (C2 tunnel)
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> any [80,443] (msg:"WebSocket upgrade with IP-based Origin - possible C2 tunnel"; flow:established,to_server; http.header; content:"Upgrade|3a 20|websocket"; nocase; http.header; content:"Origin|3a 20|http"; pcre:"/Origin\x3a\x20https?\x3a\x2f\x2f(?:[0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}/i"; sid:4000003; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect WebSocket upgrade requests with IP address in Origin header (common in C2 tools). Uses http.header for value matching with PCRE for IP pattern. Multiple http.header sticky buffers are valid for different content groups.

examples.name: Excessive WebSocket connections (C2 probing)
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET [80,443] (msg:"Excessive WebSocket connections - possible C2 infrastructure probing"; flow:established,to_client; http.stat_code; content:"101"; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 10, seconds 60; sid:4000004; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect rapid WebSocket connection establishment by counting 101 Switching Protocols responses. Uses threshold with track by_src. Do NOT use bsize after http.stat_code — status codes are always 3 digits.

examples.name: CRLF injection in HTTP headers (request smuggling)
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET [80,443] (msg:"CRLF injection in HTTP headers - request smuggling attempt"; flow:established,to_server; http.header; content:"|0d 0a|"; content:"|0d 0a|"; distance:1; within:5; sid:4000005; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect CRLF injection within HTTP header values. Uses two content:"|0d 0a|" matches with distance:1; within:5; to find injected CRLFs close together. Do NOT use content:!"|0d 0a 0d 0a|" negation — it does not work as intended. Do NOT match |0d 0a 0d 0a| alone — it naturally terminates headers.

examples.name: HTTP/2 Content-Length:0 with non-empty body (smuggling)
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (msg:"HTTP/2 Content-Length header with non-empty body - possible smuggling"; flow:established,to_server; http.protocol; content:"HTTP/2"; http.header_names; content:"Content-Length"; nocase; http.request_body; isdataat:1; sid:4000006; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect HTTP/2 requests with Content-Length header and a non-empty body (smuggling indicator). Uses http.header_names for header presence check, then http.request_body with isdataat:1 to verify body is non-empty. Do NOT use http.content_len; content:"0" — use http.header_names instead. Do NOT use content:!"|00|" for body detection — use isdataat:1.

examples.name: SMB null session enumeration (NTLMSSP Type 1)
examples.rule: alert smb $HOME_NET any -> $HOME_NET 445 (msg:"SMB null session enumeration detected - possible reconnaissance"; flow:established,to_server; content:"NTLMSSP"; content:"|01 00 00 00|"; distance:1; within:4; sid:5000001; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect NTLMSSP Negotiate (Type 1) messages for anonymous auth. NTLMSSP signature is 8 bytes, then 1 null terminator, then message type. Use distance:1; within:4; NOT distance:0.

examples.name: SMB authentication failures (STATUS_LOGON_FAILURE)
examples.rule: alert smb $HOME_NET 445 -> $HOME_NET any (msg:"Excessive SMB authentication failures - possible credential brute force"; flow:established,to_client; content:"|6d 00 00 c0|"; threshold:type threshold, track by_both, count 5, seconds 60; sid:5000002; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect SMB STATUS_LOGON_FAILURE (0xC000006D) responses. Little-endian: |6d 00 00 c0|. Responses come FROM server (port 445). Use type threshold for rate counting, NOT type both.

examples.name: SMB admin share access from non-server (smb.share)
examples.rule: alert smb !$SERVERS any -> $HOME_NET 445 (msg:"Administrative share access from workstation - possible lateral movement"; flow:established,to_server; smb.share; pcre:"/^(?:ADMIN\$|C\$|IPC\$)$/i"; sid:5000003; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect TREE_CONNECT to admin shares using smb.share sticky buffer with PCRE OR alternation. Do NOT use multiple content matches (AND logic) — use PCRE for OR. Source negation !$SERVERS excludes legitimate servers.

examples.name: PsExec-style service creation via SMB (smb.named_pipe)
examples.rule: alert smb $HOME_NET any -> $HOME_NET 445 (msg:"Suspicious remote service creation via SMB - possible PsExec lateral movement"; flow:established,to_server; smb.named_pipe; content:"svcctl"; nocase; content:"PSEXESVC"; nocase; sid:5000004; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect DCE/RPC Service Control Manager calls via svcctl named pipe. Use smb.named_pipe sticky buffer, NOT raw Unicode content. For multiple service names use PCRE: pcre:"/(?:PSEXESVC|BTOBTO|PAExec)/i";

examples.name: Rapid NTLM authentication (relay indicator)
examples.rule: alert smb $HOME_NET any -> $HOME_NET 445 (msg:"Rapid NTLM authentication attempts - possible relay attack"; flow:established,to_server; content:"NTLMSSP"; content:"|03 00 00 00|"; distance:1; within:4; threshold:type threshold, track by_both, count 3, seconds 10; sid:5000005; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect rapid NTLM Type 3 (Authentication) messages as relay indicator. True relay detection requires comparing challenge-response pairs (beyond Suricata). Use distance:1; within:4; after NTLMSSP. Use type threshold NOT type both.

examples.name: QUIC Initial packet on non-standard port (C2 evasion)
examples.rule: alert udp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET ![443,80,8443] (msg:"QUIC connection on non-standard port - possible C2 evasion"; flow:to_server; content:"|c0|"; depth:1; content:"|00 00 00 01|"; distance:4; within:4; threshold:type limit, track by_src, count 1, seconds 60; sid:6000001; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect QUIC Long Header Initial packets (0xC0 = bit 0x80 set + Initial type) with QUIC version 1 (|00 00 00 01|) on non-standard ports. Use port negation ![443,80,8443] in header.

examples.name: QUIC CRYPTO frame with suspicious SNI
examples.rule: alert udp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"QUIC connection with suspicious SNI - possible C2 domain"; flow:to_server; content:"|06|"; content:"|00 00|"; distance:0; within:50; pcre:"/\x00\x00.{2}(?:(?:[0-9]{1,3}\\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}|[0-9a-f]{32,})/"; sid:6000002; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect QUIC CRYPTO frame (type 0x06) with TLS SNI extension (0x0000) containing IP addresses or hex-encoded DGA domains. Use single content:|00 00| with within:50, NOT duplicate content matches.

examples.name: Excessive QUIC connection migrations
examples.rule: alert udp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"Excessive QUIC connection migrations - possible C2 resilience technique"; flow:to_server; content:"|18|"; threshold:type threshold, track by_both, count 10, seconds 120; sid:6000003; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect QUIC NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames (type 0x18) indicating connection migration. Use threshold by_both to track source-destination pairs.

examples.name: QUIC Short Header beaconing pattern
examples.rule: alert udp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"QUIC beaconing pattern detected - possible automated C2 check-in"; flow:to_server; dsize:<200; pcre:"/^[\\x00-\\x7f]/"; threshold:type threshold, track by_both, count 8, seconds 240; sid:6000004; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect QUIC Short Header packets (bit 0x80 NOT set) with small payloads at regular intervals. Use pcre:/^[\x00-\x7f]/ for bit-level check, NOT content:!|80| which only checks byte equality.

examples.name: MQTT CONNECT with suspicious client credentials
examples.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET [1883,8883] (msg:"MQTT connection with suspicious client credentials - possible IoT C2"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|10|"; depth:1; content:"|00 04 4d 51 54 54|"; distance:1; within:6; pcre:"/\x00\x04MQTT[\x04\x05].{4}\x00.[a-fA-F0-9]{16,}/s"; sid:7000001; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect MQTT CONNECT (0x10) with protocol identifier and hex-pattern Client ID. Handles MQTT 3.1.1 (0x04) and 5.0 (0x05). Client ID length is 2 bytes (\x00.).

examples.name: MQTT PUBLISH to encoded topic
examples.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET [1883,8883] (msg:"MQTT publish to encoded topic - possible C2 command channel"; flow:established,to_server; pcre:"/^[\x30-\x3f]/"; pcre:"/.{1,4}\x00.(?:(?:cmd|exec|shell|data)\/[A-Za-z0-9+\/=]{10,}|[A-Za-z0-9+\/=]{50,})/s"; sid:7000002; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect MQTT PUBLISH (0x30-0x3F) with encoded topic names. Topic length is 2 bytes (\x00.), NOT 1 byte. .{1,4} skips variable remaining length.

examples.name: MQTT wildcard subscription to command topics
examples.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET [1883,8883] (msg:"MQTT wildcard subscription to command topics - possible C2 listener"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|82|"; depth:1; pcre:"/(?:cmd|exec|shell|control|admin).*?[#\+]|[#\+].*?(?:cmd|exec|shell|control|admin)/i"; sid:7000003; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect MQTT SUBSCRIBE (0x82) with wildcard patterns (# or +) combined with command keywords. Bidirectional PCRE matching.

examples.name: CoAP POST to suspicious resource path
examples.rule: alert udp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET [5683,5684] (msg:"CoAP POST to suspicious resource path - possible IoT C2 command"; flow:to_server; content:"|02|"; offset:1; depth:1; pcre:"/(?:\.(?:sh|exe|bat|ps1)|[A-Za-z0-9+\/=]{100,}|[a-fA-F0-9]{40,})/s"; sid:7000004; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect CoAP POST (method code 0x02 at offset 1) with suspicious URI paths. Match POST at offset:1 to handle variable token length. Do NOT hardcode first byte.

examples.name: Excessive MQTT PINGREQ keep-alive
examples.rule: alert tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET [1883,8883] (msg:"Excessive MQTT keep-alive with minimal data - possible dormant C2 channel"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|c0 00|"; depth:2; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 20, seconds 300; sid:7000005; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect excessive MQTT PINGREQ (0xC0 + remaining length 0x00) indicating dormant C2 channel maintenance.

examples.name: Large image upload to external hosting
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"Large image upload to external hosting service - possible steganography"; flow:established,to_server; http.method; content:"POST"; http.content_type; content:"image/"; http.host; pcre:"/(?:imgur\.com|postimg\.cc|imgbb\.com)/i"; http.content_len; byte_test:0,>,5242880,0,string,dec; sid:9000001; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect large image uploads (>5MB) via POST to known image hosting services. Uses byte_test:0 (reads entire ASCII buffer as decimal) on http.content_len for size check.

examples.name: PNG with suspicious custom chunks
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"PNG with suspicious custom chunks - possible steganography container"; flow:established,to_server; http.method; content:"POST"; file_data; content:"|89 50 4E 47 0D 0A 1A 0A|"; pcre:"/(?:stEg|dAtA|sEcR|[a-z][A-Z][a-z][A-Z])/s"; sid:9000002; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect PNG files with non-standard chunk types that may hide steganographic data. Uses file_data (underscore) sticky buffer with PNG magic bytes and PCRE for suspicious chunk names.

examples.name: JPEG with excessive comment markers
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"JPEG with excessive comment markers - possible hidden data"; flow:established,to_server; http.method; content:"POST"; file_data; content:"|FF D8 FF|"; content:"|FF FE|"; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 4, seconds 60; sid:9000003; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect JPEG files with more than 3 COM markers (0xFFFE). Uses count 4 because 'more than 3' means the 4th occurrence triggers. Uses file_data for file content inspection.

examples.name: Base64-encoded image in HTTP body
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"Large base64-encoded image in HTTP body - possible steganography exfiltration"; flow:established,to_server; http.method; content:"POST"; http.content_type; pcre:"/^application\/(json|xml)/i"; file_data; content:"data:image/"; pcre:"/data:image\/(?:png|jpeg);base64,[A-Za-z0-9+\/=]{13300,}/s"; sid:9000004; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect base64-encoded image data in JSON or XML HTTP POST bodies. Uses PCRE on http.content_type to match both application/json and application/xml. Uses {13300,} because 10MB decoded ≈ 13.3MB base64.

examples.name: Repeated image uploads to same destination
examples.rule: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"Excessive image uploads to single destination - possible automated steganography exfiltration"; flow:established,to_server; http.method; content:"POST"; http.content_type; content:"image/"; threshold:type threshold, track by_both, count 10, seconds 600; sid:9000005; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect repeated image uploads (>10 in 600s) from same source to same destination using threshold with by_both tracking. Suggests automated exfiltration tool.

examples.name: DC replication pass rule (bidirectional)
examples.rule: pass tcp $AD_DOMAIN_CONTROLLERS any <> $AD_DOMAIN_CONTROLLERS [88,389,636,3268] (msg:"PASS - Allow legitimate DC-to-DC replication traffic"; flow:established; sid:1000001; rev:1;)
examples.description: Allow DC-to-DC replication on Kerberos, LDAP, LDAPS, Global Catalog ports. Uses <> bidirectional operator with flow:established (NO direction qualifier — to_server/to_client conflict with <>).

examples.name: Kerberoasting TGS-REQ with RC4-HMAC detection
examples.rule: drop tcp [$INTERNAL_NET,!$ADMIN_WORKSTATIONS] any -> $AD_DOMAIN_CONTROLLERS 88 (msg:"DROP - Block Kerberoasting TGS-REQ with RC4-HMAC etype 23"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|a0 03 02 01 05 a1 03 02 01 0c|"; content:"|a0 03 02 01 17|"; sid:1000003; rev:1;)
examples.description: Block TGS-REQ (msg type 12) with RC4-HMAC encryption (etype 23) from non-admin workstations. ASN.1: a0 03 02 01 05 = Kerberos v5, a1 03 02 01 0c = TGS-REQ, a0 03 02 01 17 = RC4-HMAC.

examples.name: DCSync attack blocking via DRSUAPI
examples.rule: drop tcp [$INTERNAL_NET,!$AD_DOMAIN_CONTROLLERS] any -> $AD_DOMAIN_CONTROLLERS [135,445] (msg:"DROP - Block DCSync DRS GetNCChanges from non-DC source"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|05 00|"; depth:2; content:"|e8 06 d0 46 9b b8 01 18 9d 68 00 80 5f 9b 4f b4|"; sid:1000007; rev:1;)
examples.description: Block DRS replication requests from non-domain controllers. Detects DRSUAPI interface UUID (e8 06 d0 46...) in DCE/RPC traffic. Only DCs should replicate.

examples.name: External Kerberos authentication rejection
examples.rule: reject tcp $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $AD_DOMAIN_CONTROLLERS 88 (msg:"REJECT - Block Kerberos auth from external networks"; flow:to_server; content:"|a0 03 02 01 05|"; depth:7; sid:1000008; rev:1;)
examples.description: Reject Kerberos authentication from external networks with TCP RST. Detects Kerberos v5 marker in first 7 bytes. Uses flow:to_server (not established) to catch initial packets.

examples.name: Silver Ticket admin share access detection
examples.rule: alert smb $INTERNAL_NET any -> $AD_DOMAIN_CONTROLLERS 445 (msg:"ALERT - Silver Ticket abuse - admin share access pattern"; flow:established,to_server; smb.share; pcre:"/^(?:ADMIN\$|C\$|IPC\$)$/i"; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 6, seconds 240; sid:1000009; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect service ticket access to admin shares without normal TGT exchange. Uses smb.share sticky buffer with PCRE alternation for OR matching. Requires Suricata 5.0+ with SMB support.

examples.name: Kerberos TGS-REQ sensitive SPN monitoring
examples.rule: alert tcp $INTERNAL_NET any -> $AD_DOMAIN_CONTROLLERS 88 (msg:"ALERT - High-value Kerberos TGS-REQ for sensitive SPNs"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|a0 03 02 01 05|"; content:"|a1 03 02 01 0c|"; pcre:"/(?:MSSQL|HTTP|CIFS|HOST)\/[a-zA-Z0-9\-\.]+/i"; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 11, seconds 180; sid:1000002; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect TGS-REQ for sensitive SPNs with threshold >10 (count 11 because 'more than 10' means the 11th triggers). Uses Kerberos v5 + TGS-REQ ASN.1 markers.

examples.name: AS-REP Roasting enumeration detection
examples.rule: alert tcp $INTERNAL_NET any -> $AD_DOMAIN_CONTROLLERS 88 (msg:"ALERT - AS-REP Roasting enumeration - bulk AS-REQ without pre-authentication"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|a0 03 02 01 05|"; content:"|a1 03 02 01 0a|"; content:!"|a7|"; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 9, seconds 90; sid:1000006; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect AS-REQ without pre-authentication (missing pa-data tag a7). content:!|a7| is CRITICAL for AS-REP Roasting detection. Threshold >8 = count 9.

examples.name: Golden Ticket detection via abnormal ticket lifetime
examples.rule: reject tcp $INTERNAL_NET any -> $AD_DOMAIN_CONTROLLERS 88 (msg:"REJECT - Suspected Golden Ticket - AS-REQ with abnormal ticket lifetime"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|a0 03 02 01 05|"; content:"|a1 03 02 01 0a|"; pcre:"/\xa5.{0,20}\x30.{0,10}\x18[\x00-\x7f]{10,17}\x18[\x00-\x7f]{10,17}/s"; sid:1000005; rev:1;)
examples.description: Detect AS-REQ with suspicious ticket lifetime fields. PCRE matches two consecutive GeneralizedTime values (tag 0x18) in the KDC-REQ-BODY (tag a5).

examples.name: AWS: URL category - block malicious URLs
examples.rule: alert http any any -> any any (msg:"URL Category is Malicious"; aws_url_category:Malicious; sid:55555555; rev:1;)
examples.description: Alert on HTTP traffic categorized as malicious using aws_url_category. Evaluates complete URLs for HTTP, requires TLS inspection for HTTPS. Falls back to domain-level if no URL match.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall URL/Domain Category Filtering (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: URL category - block gambling and social networking
examples.rule: drop http any any -> any any (msg:"Block gambling and social sites"; aws_url_category:Gambling,Social Networking; sid:55555556; rev:1;)
examples.description: Block HTTP traffic to gambling and social networking sites. Multiple categories can be specified comma-separated in a single rule.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall URL/Domain Category Filtering (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: Domain category - alert on cryptocurrency domains
examples.rule: alert tls any any -> any any (msg:"Domain Category is Cryptocurrency"; aws_domain_category:Cryptocurrency; sid:55555557; rev:1;)
examples.description: Alert on TLS traffic to cryptocurrency domains using aws_domain_category. Evaluates domain from TLS SNI field. No TLS inspection required.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall URL/Domain Category Filtering (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: Domain category - block malware and phishing
examples.rule: reject tls $HOME_NET any -> any any (msg:"Category:Malware,Phishing"; aws_domain_category:Malware,Phishing; flow:to_server; sid:202602065; rev:1;)
examples.description: Block TLS connections to domains categorized as Malware or Phishing. CANNOT combine aws_domain_category with geoip in the same rule — use separate rules.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall URL/Domain Category Filtering (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: GeoIP negation - drop traffic not from US or UK
examples.rule: drop ip any any -> any any (msg:"Drop traffic not from US or UK"; geoip:src,!US,UK; sid:55555558; rev:1;)
examples.description: Drop traffic from any country except US and UK using geoip negation. Negation applies to the first country code, remaining are included.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Geographic IP Filtering (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: HTTP/2 domain filtering - allow specific domain
examples.rule: pass http2 $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (http.request_header; content:"authority|3a 20|example.com"; sid:1; rev:1;)
examples.description: Allow HTTP/2 traffic to specific domain using http.request_header with authority pseudo-header. Requires TLS inspection to decrypt HTTP/2.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: HTTP/2 block non-allowed decrypted traffic
examples.rule: drop http2 $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (flow:established, to_server; http2.header_name; content:"authority"; sid:3; rev:1;)
examples.description: Drop all HTTP/2 decrypted traffic not matching allowed domains. Uses http2.header_name sticky buffer to match the authority header.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: Reject non-HTTP/2 TCP traffic
examples.rule: reject tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (app-layer-protocol:!http2; flow:to_server, established; sid:2; rev:1;)
examples.description: Reject TCP traffic that is not HTTP/2 protocol. Used in combination with HTTP/2 domain filtering rules.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: SNI allowlist with xbits cert issuer verification
examples.rule: alert tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (ssl_state:client_hello; tls.sni; content:"checkip.amazonaws.com"; endswith; nocase; xbits:set, allowed_sni_destination_ips, track ip_dst, expire 3600; noalert; sid:238745;)
examples.description: Set xbit on allowed SNI for subsequent certificate issuer verification. Uses noalert to suppress alert while setting the xbit. Part of multi-rule pattern.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: xbits stateless pass for allowed destinations
examples.rule: pass tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET 443 (xbits:isset, allowed_sni_destination_ips, track ip_dst; flow:stateless; sid:89207006;)
examples.description: Pass TCP traffic to destinations with allowed xbit set. Uses flow:stateless to match packets regardless of connection state. Part of SNI+cert issuer verification pattern.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: Certificate issuer verification pass rule
examples.rule: pass tls $EXTERNAL_NET 443 -> $HOME_NET any (tls.cert_issuer; content:"Amazon"; msg:"Pass rules do not alert"; xbits:isset, allowed_sni_destination_ips, track ip_src; sid:29822;)
examples.description: Pass TLS traffic from allowed destinations only if certificate issuer is Amazon. Uses xbits:isset to verify the SNI was previously allowed.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: Block non-Amazon cert issuers for allowed SNIs
examples.rule: reject tls $EXTERNAL_NET 443 -> $HOME_NET any (tls.cert_issuer; content:"="; nocase; msg:"Block all other cert issuers not allowed by sid:29822"; sid:897972;)
examples.description: Reject TLS traffic from allowed SNI destinations if certificate issuer is not Amazon. Catches certificate impersonation attacks.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: SSH/SFTP banner filtering - allow AWS Transfer Family
examples.rule: pass ssh $EXTERNAL_NET 22 -> $HOME_NET any (ssh.software; content:"AWS_SFTP"; flow:from_server; sid:217872;)
examples.description: Allow only SSH/SFTP servers with AWS_SFTP banner (AWS Transfer Family). Uses ssh.software sticky buffer with flow:from_server to match server banner.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: Block unauthorized SSH servers
examples.rule: drop ssh $EXTERNAL_NET 22 -> $HOME_NET any (ssh.software; content:!"@"; pcre:"/[a-z]/i"; msg:"Block unauthorized SFTP/SSH."; flow:from_server; sid:999217872;)
examples.description: Block SSH servers that do not have the expected banner. Uses content negation and PCRE to match any non-approved SSH software string.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: Strict order SNI allow with default drop
examples.rule: pass tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (ssl_state:client_hello; tls.sni; content:"example.com"; dotprefix; nocase; flow:to_server, established; sid:2;)
examples.description: Allow TLS traffic to specific domain in strict order mode. Must be placed BEFORE the drop rule. Uses dotprefix for subdomain matching.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: Strict order default drop for established traffic
examples.rule: drop tcp $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (flow:to_server, established; sid:1;)
examples.description: Drop all established outbound TCP traffic. In strict order mode, this must be placed AFTER pass rules. Catches all traffic not explicitly allowed.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: IP set reference with prefix list
examples.rule: drop tcp @BETA any -> any any (sid:1;)
examples.description: Block traffic from IP addresses in a VPC prefix list. The @BETA variable references a prefix list ARN defined in the rule group's ReferenceSets.IPSetReferences.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Suricata Examples (official docs)

examples.name: AWS: TLS domain deny list (auto-generated pattern)
examples.rule: drop tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (ssl_state:client_hello; tls.sni; content:"evil.com"; startswith; nocase; endswith; msg:"matching TLS denylisted FQDNs"; flow:to_server, established; sid:1; rev:1;)
examples.description: Strict-order equivalent of the pattern Network Firewall generates for TLS domain deny lists. Uses ssl_state:client_hello, startswith+endswith for exact FQDN match. Place before any default allow rules.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Domain List Generated Rules (adapted for strict order)

examples.name: AWS: TLS domain allow list with default drop (auto-generated pattern)
examples.rule: drop tls $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"not matching any TLS allowlisted FQDNs"; ssl_state:client_hello; flow:to_server, established; sid:3; rev:1;)
examples.description: Strict-order equivalent of the default drop rule for TLS domain allow lists. Drops all TLS traffic not matching any allowed domain. Place after all pass rules.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Domain List Generated Rules (adapted for strict order)

examples.name: AWS: HTTP domain allow list with wildcard (auto-generated pattern)
examples.rule: pass http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (http.host; dotprefix; content:".amazon.com"; endswith; msg:"matching HTTP allowlisted FQDNs"; flow:to_server, established; sid:1; rev:1;)
examples.description: Strict-order equivalent of the pattern Network Firewall generates for HTTP domain allow lists with wildcard. Uses dotprefix for subdomain matching. Place before default drop rules.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Domain List Generated Rules (adapted for strict order)

examples.name: AWS: HTTP allow list default drop (auto-generated pattern)
examples.rule: drop http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (http.header_names; content:"|0d 0a|"; startswith; msg:"not matching any HTTP allowlisted FQDNs"; flow:to_server, established; sid:3; rev:1;)
examples.description: Strict-order equivalent of the default drop rule for HTTP domain allow lists. Uses http.header_names with CRLF content to match any HTTP request with headers. Place after all pass rules.
examples.source: AWS Network Firewall Domain List Generated Rules (adapted for strict order)
